content:
# Joshua Chapter 19 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 242-244 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_019_page_001.jpg`

1. And the second lot came out for Shimon, for the tribe of the sons of Shimon according to their clans; and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Yehudah.

2. And they had in their inheritance Beer-Sheva and Sheva and Moladah,

3. and Chatzar-Shual and Balah and Etzem,

4. and Eltolad and Betul and Chormah,

5. and Tziqlag and Bet-ha-Markavot and Chatzar-Susah,

6. and Bet-Levot and Sharuchen — thirteen cities and their villages;

7. Ayin, Rimmon, and Eter, and Ashan — four cities and their villages;

8. and all the villages which [were] around these cities as far as Baalat-Beer, Ramat of the Negev. This [was] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Shimon according to their clans.

9. From the allotment of the sons of Yehudah [was] the inheritance of the sons of Shimon, for the portion of the sons of Yehudah was too great for them; and the sons of Shimon received [their] inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.

10. And the third lot came up for the sons of Zevulun according to their clans; and the border of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.

11. And their border went up westward and [to] Maralah, and reached to Dabbeshet, and reached to the wadi which [is] before Yoqneam.

12. And it turned from Sarid eastward, [toward the] rising of the sun, to the border of Kislot-Tavor, and went out to Daverat, and went up [to] Yaphia.

13. And from there it passed eastward to the east, to Gat-Chepher, to Et-Qatzin, and went out [to] Rimmon, [which] turns to Neah.

14. And the border went around it on the north [to] Channaton, and its goings out were the valley of Yiphtach-El.

15. And Qattat and Nahalal and Shimron and Yidalah and Bet-Lechem — twelve cities and their villages.

16. This [was] the inheritance of the sons of Zevulun according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

17. For Yissakhar the fourth lot came out, for the sons of Yissakhar according to their clans.

18. And their border was [to] Yizreel and the Kesullot and Shunem,

19. and Chapharayim and Shion and Anacharat,

20. and Rabbit and Qishyon and Avetz,

21. and Remet and En-Gannim and En-Chaddah and Bet-Patzetz.

22. And the border reached to Tavor and Shachatzimah and Bet-Shemesh, and the goings out of their border were at the Yarden — sixteen cities and their villages.

23. This [was] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Yissakhar according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

24. And the fifth lot came out for the tribe of the sons of Asher according to their clans.

25. And their border was Chelqat and Chali and Beten and Akhshaph,

26. and Allammelekh and Amad and Mishal; and it reached to Karmel westward and to Shichor-Livnat.

27. And it turned [toward the] rising of the sun [to] Bet-Dagon, and reached to Zevulun and to the valley of Yiphtach-El northward, Bet-ha-Emeq and Neiel, and went out to Kavul on the left,

28. and Evron and Rechov and Chammon and Qanah, as far as great Tzidon.

29. And the border turned [to] Ramah and as far as the fortified city of Tzor, and the border turned [to] Chosah, and its goings out were at the sea, from the region of Akhziv.

30. And Ummah and Apheq and Rechov — twenty-two cities and their villages.

31. This [was] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Asher according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

32. For the sons of Naphtali the sixth lot came out, for the sons of Naphtali according to their clans.

33. And their border was from Cheleph, from the oak in Tzaanannim, and Adami-ha-Neqev and Yavneel, as far as Laqqum, and its goings out were at the Yarden.

34. And the border turned westward [to] Aznot-Tavor, and went out from there [to] Chuqoq, and reached to Zevulun on the south, and to Asher it reached on the west, and to Yehudah at the Yarden [toward the] rising of the sun.

35. And the fortified cities [were] ha-Tziddim, Tzer, and Chammat, Raqqat, and Kinneret,

36. and Adamah and Ramah and Chatzor,

37. and Qedesh and Edrei and En-Chatzor,

38. and Yiron and Migdal-El, Chorem and Bet-Anat and Bet-Shemesh — nineteen cities and their villages.

39. This [was] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

40. For the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their clans the seventh lot came out.

41. And the border of their inheritance was Tzorah and Eshtaol and Ir-Shemesh,

42. and Shaalabbin and Ayyalon and Yitlah,

43. and Elon and Timnah and Eqron,

44. and Elteqeh and Gibbeton and Baalat,

45. and Yehud and Benei-Beraq and Gat-Rimmon,

46. and Mei-ha-Yarqon and ha-Raqqon, with the border opposite Yapho.

47. And the border of the sons of Dan went out from them, and the sons of Dan went up and fought against Leshem, and captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it and dwelt in it, and called Leshem Dan, according to the name of Dan their father.

48. This [was] the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

49. And they finished dividing the land [as] an inheritance by its borders; and the sons of Yisrael gave an inheritance to Yehoshua son of Nun in their midst.

50. By the word of YHWH they gave him the city which he asked [for], Timnat-Serach in the hill country of Ephrayim; and he built the city and dwelt in it.

51. These [are] the inheritances which Elazar the priest and Yehoshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Yisrael divided by lot in Shiloh before YHWH, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting; and they finished dividing the land.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v1 — "Shimon" (שִׁמְעוֹן):** Transliterated from Hebrew; the name means "hearing" or "he has heard."

2. **v2 — "Beer-Sheva and Sheva" (בְּאֵר שֶׁבַע וּשְׁבַע):** The MT lists both Beer-Sheva and Sheva as separate entries. The LXX treats Sheva as part of the compound name. We follow the MT division.

3. **v6 — "thirteen cities":** The count of thirteen includes cities listed across verses 2-6. Some commentators note difficulty reconciling the number with the names enumerated, as the count varies depending on whether Beer-Sheva and Sheva are one city or two.

4. **v7 — "Ayin, Rimmon" (עַיִן רִמּוֹן):** The MT presents these as two separate city names. In 1 Chronicles 4:32 and Nehemiah 11:29, they appear as the compound "En-Rimmon." We preserve the MT reading.

5. **v9 — "too great" (רַב):** Literally "much" or "abundant." The portion of Yehudah was larger than needed, hence Shimon's inheritance was carved from within it.

6. **v12 — "rising of the sun" (מִזְרַח הַשֶּׁמֶשׁ):** Hebrew idiom for "eastward," preserved literally per EMET rules.

7. **v13 — "Gat-Chepher" (גִּתָּה חֵפֶר):** The birthplace of the prophet Yonah (2 Kings 14:25). The directional he on גִּתָּה indicates motion toward.

8. **v15 — "twelve cities":** The enumerated names in verses 10-15 do not straightforwardly yield twelve. Ancient counting may have included unnamed settlements or sub-divisions within the border description.

9. **v22 — "sixteen cities":** As with other tribal allotments, the stated number and the enumerated names require careful counting; some border-description towns may be included in the tally.

10. **v27 — "on the left" (מִשְּׂמֹאל):** "Left" in Hebrew orientation (facing east) means "north." Kavul was north of the line of description.

11. **v29 — "Chosah" (חֹסָה):** Some identify this with Uzu, the mainland settlement of Tyre.

12. **v33 — "the oak in Tzaanannim" (אֵלוֹן בְּצַעֲנַנִּים):** Also referenced in Judges 4:11. The word אֵלוֹן can mean "oak" or "terebinth."

13. **v34 — "to Yehudah at the Yarden":** This difficult phrase has generated much discussion. Some suggest a Yehudah enclave east of the main tribal territory, others emend the text. We follow the MT as written.

14. **v35 — "ha-Tziddim" (הַצִּדִּים):** The definite article is preserved. The name may mean "the sides" or "the fortifications."

15. **v41 — "Ir-Shemesh" (עִיר שֶׁמֶשׁ):** Literally "City of the Sun," likely identical with Bet-Shemesh ("House of the Sun").

16. **v46 — "Mei-ha-Yarqon" (מֵי הַיַּרְקוֹן):** Literally "Waters of the Yarqon," referring to the river near modern Tel Aviv.

17. **v47 — "went out from them" (וַיֵּצֵא...מֵהֶם):** The Hebrew indicates that Dan's territory "was too constrained for them" — their allotted border was insufficient, prompting the migration north to Leshem. This episode is narrated fully in Judges 18.

18. **v50 — "Timnat-Serach" (תִּמְנַת סֶרַח):** In Judges 2:9 the name appears as "Timnat-Cherес" (תִּמְנַת חֶרֶס) with reversed consonants. The Leningrad Codex reads Serach here.

19. **v51 — "Tent of Meeting" (אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד):** The tabernacle at Shiloh, where the lot-casting ceremony was conducted under priestly supervision. This verse forms an inclusio with Joshua 18:1, framing the entire allotment narrative.

20. **Conjunctions preserved:** The repeated use of "and" (וְ) at the beginning of nearly every verse reflects the Hebrew waw-consecutive narrative chain, preserved without smoothing per EMET method.
